colorMeta._canonicalSource: Hex tokens in DESIGN.md frontmatter are canonical. OKLCH values here are derived via deterministic sRGB->OKLCH conversion (D65, OKLab spec). Tonal ramps are anchored at the canonical L value, with chroma falling off near both lightness extremes.

error-active.comment: Lifted from #D43F3F to clear AA with text-inverse (4.6x) on the depressed-button surface.

control-badge-text.comment: The legibility-tuned text color for control badges. Canonical control violet (#9A78D6) cannot clear AA against its own 14% tint (3.7x); this brighter ramp step does (5.5x). Badge text only — borders, fills, and chip surfaces still use canonical control.

text-subtle.comment: Lifted from #686C88 to clear WCAG 2.2 AA on bg-elevated (was 3.0x; now 4.6x). Used for placeholder and tertiary text.

display.purpose: Empty states, wizard intros, hero numerics. Rare.

title.purpose: Section headings, panel titles, dialog title.

emphasized-body.purpose: Button labels, card titles, dialog description. The 16/600 step between title and body.

body.purpose: Default inputs, default labels, default prose. Cap at 65–75ch where prose runs.

label.purpose: Helper text, badges, dense cells, status-bar text. Adjacent to a caption at 11/400, the +500 weight delta carries the hierarchy.

caption.purpose: Tabular captions, tick labels, kbd chips. The smallest legitimate text.

mono.purpose: Live numerics: number-input fields, axis readouts, calibration thresholds, count badges.

shadows.name: shadow-0
shadows.purpose: Default for almost every surface. Layering comes from luminance, not blur.

shadows.name: shadow-1
shadows.purpose: Chamfer highlight. A 1px light bloom on the upper edge of an elevated panel.

shadows.name: shadow-2
shadows.purpose: Stamped-panel. 1px hard ridge below plus chamfer above. No blur. Used by Card.

shadows.name: shadow-3
shadows.purpose: Genuine overlay. Reserved for menus, tooltips, dialogs, toasts. Nothing else.

motion.name: duration-fast
motion.purpose: Hover, focus, press, opacity fade, colour transition. Anything synchronous with the input event.

motion.name: duration-base
motion.purpose: Tab indicator slide, toast entry transform, dialog backdrop fade.

motion.name: duration-slow
motion.purpose: Dialog open scale + opacity, larger panel enter/exit. Reserved for spatial transitions large enough to want a touch of weight.

motion.name: easing-fast
motion.purpose: Ease-out-expo. The synchronous curve. Used for every <=180ms transition on hover, focus, press, opacity, colour. Response begins immediately with the input event.

motion.name: easing-standard
motion.purpose: The 'needle finding its mark' curve. Slight initial dwell, clean decelerate. Used only for >=240ms container enter/exit (Dialog open, Toast slide). At <=180ms the dwell reads as latency; use easing-fast instead.

motion.name: easing-emphasized
motion.purpose: Sharper decelerate for accented moments (alarm reveal, mode switch confirmation).

motion.name: easing-linear
motion.purpose: Only when the animation IS the data: live axis indicators, real-metric progress. Also the easing under prefers-reduced-motion. Never on chrome.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single action color in the system. Used sparingly per The One Action Color Rule.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; padding: 8px 16px; background: var(--color-primary, #4FA8FF); color: var(--color-primary-fg, #06101F); border: 1px solid var(--color-primary, #4FA8FF); border-radius: 4px; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 14px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 100ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), border-color 100ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--color-primary-hover, #6FBAFF); border-color: var(--color-primary-hover, #6FBAFF); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-border-focus, #5AB0FF); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); background: var(--color-primary-active, #2D7FD9); border-color: var(--color-primary-active, #2D7FD9); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Default action surface. Strong border carries the affordance, not a fill.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; padding: 8px 16px; background: var(--color-bg-elevated, #1E223A); color: var(--color-text, #E4E6F0); border: 1px solid var(--color-border-strong, #424766); border-radius: 4px; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 14px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 100ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), border-color 100ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: var(--color-border-focus, #5AB0FF); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-border-focus, #5AB0FF); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-secondary:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Transparent at rest. Hover fills with elevated-navy plus a hairline so the affordance reads on dark surfaces.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; padding: 8px 16px; background: transparent; color: var(--color-text, #E4E6F0); border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 4px; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 14px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 100ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), border-color 100ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: var(--color-bg-elevated, #1E223A); border-color: var(--color-border, #2A2E48); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-border-focus, #5AB0FF); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Flat-with-hairline by default. Cards do not float; surface luminance plus 1px border carries the lift.
components.html:
Mapping
Stick X axis routes to vJoy 1 axis X with a curve and an outer deadzone.
components.css: .ds-card { background: var(--color-bg-elevated, #1E223A); border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #2A2E48); border-radius: 4px; padding: 16px; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; color: var(--color-text, #E4E6F0); } .ds-card__title { font-size: 16px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.45; margin-bottom: 8px; } .ds-card__body { font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.45; color: var(--color-text-muted, #9CA0BC); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Sunken navy reads as recessed. Focus raises border to focus-cyan with a 2px outline.
components.css: .ds-input { display: inline-block; border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #2A2E48); border-radius: 4px; background: var(--color-bg-sunken, #0E1020); color: var(--color-text, #E4E6F0); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; padding: 8px 12px; transition: border-color 100ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--color-text-subtle, #8589A7); } .ds-input:hover:not(:disabled) { border-color: var(--color-border-strong, #424766); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-border-focus, #5AB0FF); outline-offset: 2px; border-color: var(--color-border-focus, #5AB0FF); }

components.name: Number Input
components.description: Mono numerals, right-aligned, 6ch wide field with vertical steppers separated by a hairline. Native spinners hidden cross-browser.
components.css: .ds-num { display: inline-flex; align-items: stretch; border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #2A2E48); border-radius: 4px; background: var(--color-bg-sunken, #0E1020); overflow: hidden; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-num:focus-within { outline: 2px solid var(--color-border-focus, #5AB0FF); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-num__field { border: none; background: transparent; color: var(--color-text, #E4E6F0); font-family: JetBrainsMono, ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 14px; font-feature-settings: "tnum" 1, "ss03" 1; padding: 8px 12px; width: 6ch; text-align: right; } .ds-num__field:focus { outline: none; } .ds-num__steppers { display: flex; flex-direction: column; border-left: 1px solid var(--color-border, #2A2E48); } .ds-num__step { width: 22px; height: 50%; background: transparent; border: none; color: var(--color-text-muted, #9CA0BC); cursor: pointer; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; transition: background 100ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), color 100ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-num__step + .ds-num__step { border-top: 1px solid var(--color-border, #2A2E48); } .ds-num__step:hover { background: var(--color-bg-elevated, #1E223A); color: var(--color-text, #E4E6F0); }

components.name: Switch
components.description: Sunken track flips to primary on check. Thumb travel is derived from token math so resizing the track propagates.
components.css: .ds-switch { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; color: var(--color-text, #E4E6F0); } .ds-switch__input { position: absolute; opacity: 0; pointer-events: none; } .ds-switch__track { position: relative; width: 36px; height: 20px; background: var(--color-bg-sunken, #0E1020); border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #2A2E48); border-radius: 9999px; transition: background 100ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-switch__thumb { position: absolute; top: 1px; left: 1px; width: 16px; height: 16px; background: var(--color-text-muted, #9CA0BC); border-radius: 9999px; transition: transform 100ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), background 100ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-switch__input:checked ~ .ds-switch__track { background: var(--color-primary, #4FA8FF); border-color: var(--color-primary, #4FA8FF); } .ds-switch__input:checked ~ .ds-switch__track .ds-switch__thumb { transform: translateX(16px); background: var(--color-primary-fg, #06101F); } .ds-switch__input:focus-visible ~ .ds-switch__track { outline: 2px solid var(--color-border-focus, #5AB0FF); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Tabs
components.description: Etched-selector tabs. The 3px primary underline overlaps the 1px tablist border so the indicator anchors to the panel edge rather than floating above it.
components.css: .ds-tabs { display: flex; flex-direction: row; align-items: stretch; gap: 4px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--color-border-strong, #424766); padding: 0 4px; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-tab { appearance: none; background: transparent; border: 0; border-bottom: 3px solid transparent; color: var(--color-text-muted, #9CA0BC); font: inherit; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.01em; padding: 8px 12px; margin-bottom: -1px; cursor: pointer; transition: color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), border-color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-tab:hover { color: var(--color-text, #E4E6F0); } .ds-tab--active { color: var(--color-text, #E4E6F0); border-bottom-color: var(--color-primary, #4FA8FF); } .ds-tab:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-border-focus, #5AB0FF); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: 2px; }

components.name: Status Bar
components.description: Telemetry strip at the bottom of the shell. Sunken navy plus a 1px strong border on top reads as recessed instrument readout.
components.html:
Running
Mode Combat
2/2 devices
components.css: .ds-statusbar { display: flex; align-items: center; height: 28px; padding: 0 12px; border-top: 1px solid var(--color-border-strong, #424766); background: var(--color-bg-sunken, #0E1020); color: var(--color-text-muted, #9CA0BC); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; gap: 12px; } .ds-statusbar__slot { display: flex; flex-direction: row; align-items: center; gap: 8px; min-width: 0; } .ds-statusbar__slot--mid { flex: 1; } .ds-dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 9999px; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-dot--live { background: var(--color-live, #2EE0A0); box-shadow: 0 0 6px -2px var(--color-live, #2EE0A0); } .ds-mode { color: var(--color-control, #9A78D6); font-weight: 500; }

components.name: Annunciator Toast
components.description: Signature component. Backlit cockpit indicator with a chamfer-aligned 3px left accent stripe and a level-tinted surface.
components.html: HidHide unavailable. Falling back to direct passthrough.3
components.css: .ds-toast { pointer-events: auto; position: relative; background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--color-warning, #FFB347) 8%, var(--color-bg-elevated, #1E223A)); color: var(--color-warning, #FFB347); border: 1px solid var(--color-border, #2A2E48); border-radius: 4px; padding: 12px 12px 12px 15px; box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.55), 0 0 0 1px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.04); font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.15; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; max-width: 380px; } .ds-toast::before { content: ""; position: absolute; left: 0; top: 8px; bottom: 8px; width: 3px; border-radius: 0 2px 2px 0; background: currentColor; box-shadow: 0 0 8px -2px currentColor; } .ds-toast__icon { flex-shrink: 0; color: currentColor; } .ds-toast__msg { color: var(--color-text, #E4E6F0); flex: 1; min-width: 0; line-height: 1.45; } .ds-toast__count { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 9999px; background: color-mix(in oklch, currentColor 14%, transparent); border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklch, currentColor 28%, transparent); color: currentColor; font-family: JetBrainsMono, ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; font-feature-settings: "tnum" 1; letter-spacing: 0.02em; line-height: 1; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-toast__close { flex-shrink: 0; width: 22px; height: 22px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: transparent; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 2px; color: var(--color-text-subtle, #8589A7); cursor: pointer; font-size: 18px; line-height: 1; padding: 0; } .ds-toast__close:hover { color: var(--color-text, #E4E6F0); background: color-mix(in oklch, currentColor 12%, transparent); border-color: var(--color-border, #2A2E48); }

narrative.overview: InputForge's interface is an instrument-panel for a sim-input pipeline. Every surface borrows from the visual grammar of avionics multi-function displays and software DAW chrome: precise typography, deep navy-tinted neutrals, HUD-blue actions, CRT-phosphor green for live signal, taxonomy hues that stay subordinate to alerts. Density is high because the audience is high. Hierarchy and contrast carry the weight, never decoration. The system is *evolved* glass cockpit because it deliberately rejects the literal-skeumorphic cockpit (rivets, brushed-metal bezels, fake chamfers as ornament) in favor of a flat, panel-luminance-layered surface where chamfers exist as 1px inset highlights, not raised plastic. It also rejects the CRT-as-aesthetic move (scanlines, glow bloom, retro-amber-monochrome) because that nostalgia treats the instrument as decoration.
keyCharacteristics: Layering by luminance, not by shadow.
Color used as signal, never as garnish.
Inter with deliberate OpenType (tnum, ss03, cv11).
Cockpit-brisk motion. easing-fast (ease-out-expo) for <=180ms hover/focus/press; easing-standard (slight initial dwell) for >=240ms container enter/exit. No bounce, no elastic, no overshoot.
Inline-first editing surfaces. The cure for the JoystickGremlin Tk anti-reference is editing in place — Dialog is reserved for dirty-state, destructive, and OS-owned modals only.

rules.name: The One Action Color Rule
rules.body: HUD Cyan-Blue is the only action color in the system. There is no second action color. There is no accent color.

rules.name: The Subordinate Categories Rule
rules.body: Processing teal, output gold, and control violet are deliberately desaturated relative to the alert palette. They are never used at full chroma, never used for actions, and never used as decoration.

rules.name: The No Pure Black, No Pure White Rule
rules.body: All neutrals are tinted toward the navy hue. #000 and #fff are forbidden anywhere in component CSS.

rules.name: The Tabular Figures Rule
rules.body: Numeric values that line up in a column use font-feature-settings 'tnum' 1.

rules.name: The Mono For Live Numerics Rule
rules.body: JetBrainsMono is reserved for inputs and readouts whose value the engine actually owns. UI labels and prose stay Inter. Mono is not a stylistic choice; it is a precision affordance.

rules.name: The 65ch Rule
rules.body: Any prose longer than two lines is capped at 65–75ch.

rules.name: The Cards Don't Float Rule
rules.body: A Card uses shadow-2 (stamped) or shadow-0 (flat). It never uses shadow-3.

rules.name: The Real Float Rule
rules.body: shadow-3 is permitted only on a surface that genuinely floats above the canvas: a menu list, a tooltip bubble, a dialog panel, a toast item.

rules.name: The Glassmorphism Exception Rule
rules.body: Tooltips are the single permitted use of backdrop-filter: blur(). Nothing else gets backdrop-filter.

rules.name: The Sub-Pixel Curve Rule
rules.body: easing-standard has a tiny ease-in (Y at P1 = 0.08). At >=240ms it reads as the panel settling on its detent. At <=180ms it reads as latency between the user's gesture and the response. For hover/focus/press transitions, use easing-fast.

rules.name: The No Bounce Rule
rules.body: No bounce, no elastic, no overshoot. easing-fast and easing-standard are the entire vocabulary. If a component spec calls for spring physics, the spec is wrong.

rules.name: The Inline-First Rule
rules.body: If a property has a single editable value, it is edited where it is displayed. The dialog opening on click is the canonical Tk failure; the system rejects it as a default. A dialog is justified only when this rule has been actively considered and rejected for a stated reason.

rules.name: The No Modal-On-Modal Rule
rules.body: A Dialog never opens another Dialog. If a confirmation is needed inside a flow already inside a Dialog, the flow itself is too deep — flatten it to an in-shell surface.

dos: Do layer surfaces by luminance plus a 1px hairline. Never by drop shadow.
Do use shadow-3 only on surfaces that genuinely float (menu, tooltip, dialog, toast).
Do keep numeric values in a tabular column on JetBrainsMono with tnum enabled.
Do keep the action color singular. HUD Cyan-Blue is the only action color.
Do carry status through shape and label, not color alone.
Do raise inactive elements to bright text on hover.
Do put real device names and real input addresses on the screen.
Do keep tightened leading (1.15 for headers, 1.45 for body) for instrument-cluster density.
donts: Don't use #000 or #fff anywhere. All neutrals tint toward the navy hue.

Don't add side-stripe borders (border-left or border-right greater than 1px) to cards, list items, callouts, or panels. The Toast accent stripe is the one documented exception — it is the peripheral-vision channel for an ambient corner-of-viewport surface. Other surfaces sit in the foveal field and have no peripheral-channel argument; the rule does not extend.
Don't apply gradient text (background-clip: text plus a gradient).
Don't use backdrop-filter outside of Tooltip.
Don't float Cards with soft drop shadows.
Don't let category hues compete with status hues.
Don't add bounce, elastic, or overshoot to motion. easing-fast for <=180ms transitions, easing-standard for >=240ms container enter/exit.
Don't render a caption (11/400) and a label (12/500) at the same weight when they sit adjacent. Weight contrast is what carries the hierarchy in the dense range.

Don't use big number cards, soft pastel shadows, friendly empty-state illustrations, marketing-style stat tiles, or 'Welcome back' greetings. Empty states use Display-typography numerics or instrument-style 'no signal' framings — never SVG illustrations.
Don't use cyan glows, RGB edge lighting, gradient angles, or 'esports' type.
Don't use grey system-default forms, modal-after-modal flows, dialog-soup confirmation chains, or Tk-era widget chrome.
Don't wrap every region in a Card.
Don't use modals for confirmations that can be inline. See section 6 Surfaces for inline / expanding-row / side-panel / dialog assignment and the list of forbidden dialog uses.
